clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for pre-operative general surgery review.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common General Surgery documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around pre-operative general surgery review.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
red_flag_prompts: severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present

chips.text: Pre-operative general surgery review interval history documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
change since last review documented
severity/impact on function documented if discussed
associated symptoms reviewed if relevant
relevant negatives documented if assessed
patient concerns or goals documented if discussed
clinician-led risk context documented
scope limited to documentation scaffolding
Vital signs documented only if assessed
Focused surgical site or abdominal examination documented only if assessed
Wound/stoma/drain documentation if present documented only if assessed
General appearance documented only if assessed
severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present
Existing imaging report reviewed if available
Laboratory results reviewed if already ordered
Operative or discharge documentation reviewed if available
clinician-entered documentation plan recorded
senior/specialist discussion documented if already performed
risk/consent discussion documented if clinician completed it
handover or follow-up documentation recorded if arranged by clinician

history_draft.text: Pre-operative general surgery review. [Interval history]. [Current symptoms/concerns]. [Change since last review]. [Relevant negatives]. [Clinician-documented discussion]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Vital signs documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Focused surgical site or abdominal examination documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Wound/stoma/drain documentation if present documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. General appearance documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Existing imaging report reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Laboratory results reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Operative or discharge documentation reviewed.

plan_options.text: Risk/consent discussion documented if completed by clinician.
Clinician-entered Handover or follow-up documentation recorded.

clinical_blockers: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.

Procedure and indication, operative options, complete risk assessment, examination, tests, medication instructions, blood, fasting, optimization, consent, scheduling and multidisciplinary decisions remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for preoperative general surgery review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
